response.justification: The role centers on hands-on C++/STL/OOP development for high-performance industrial automation platforms with secure engineering under IEC 62443, and the candidate has only ~6 months of C/C++ work (National Instruments, 2014-15) plus a university thesis. The skills list does not include C++. There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers, industrial protocols, or Secure Development Lifecycle/IEC 62443 experience. The candidate is a strong, senior full-stack engineer (TypeScript/Node.js/DLang) with relevant low-latency, multi-threaded, and event-driven distributed-systems work, but this is a poor match for a C++/industrial-cybersecurity mandate.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency, multi-threaded, high-throughput distributed-systems work (millions of HTTP req/s)
Senior/Staff-level seniority with 12+ years and strong testing/agile/CI-CD craftsmanship
Node.js, Docker, and event-driven architecture experience (advantageous skills)
concerns: Minimal C++ exposure (~6 months) and C++ absent from listed skills
No IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial-cybersecurity experience
No MQTT, message-broker, or industrial-protocol (Modbus/OPC UA/etc.) background
